50s: The most significant hurdle women faced in athletics was a general public who felt that women shouldn’t be given professional positions and that it didn’t fit their ideal domestic woman ideas. Those who did often were forced to show a “feminine ideal” which is now associated with apologetic behavior, which hindered their abilities to be competitive. The few female leagues ensured that the league fit the cult of domesticity narrative of the time, in order to even keep a small audience and not be branded as radicals. As a result, female athletes faced far more financial issues compared to their male counterparts due to a lack of engagement with sports in the 50s.
